title: Why Your YouTube Shorts Stopped Getting Views (Fix This)
description:
Why do YouTube Shorts suddenly stop getting views? If your YouTube Shorts are getting 0 views or your YouTube Shorts views dropped, there's one metric you need to check: VVSA (Views vs. Swiped Away). This measures how many people actually watch your Short versus how many swipe right past it.
If your VVSA is under 60%, people weren't grabbed by the first thing they saw, so they swiped away. Here's what most creators miss: your first frame IS your thumbnail on YouTube Shorts. On long-form videos, people click a thumbnail then watch. On Shorts, they're already watching, so the very first image that appears on screen decides if someone stays or swipes.
Most creators start with a blank wall, their face mid-blink, or nothing happening. What works? Movement, action, something unexpected. Start in the middle of the action with an attention-grabbing object in frame. This is how to increase YouTube Shorts views and fix YouTube Shorts getting no views.
Check your VVSA in YouTube Studio Analytics. If you're under 60%, your first frame needs work. This simple fix can transform your Shorts performance.
